skill.name: test-driven-development
skill.description: Guides AI coding assistants through strict test-driven development before implementation work begins.
skill.summary: Apply red-green-refactor discipline to features, bug fixes, and refactoring work.
skill.author: Cycleaddict

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown code fences, test snippets, and keyword matches as command execution, weak cryptography, or reconnaissance. Manual review found documentation-only TDD guidance with benign npm test examples and no evidence of scripts, network access, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Benign Test Command Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown fences, TypeScript examples, and npm test snippets used to explain the TDD workflow. The skill contains no executable scripts and does not run commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows documentation and examples rather than callable code. The only real shell examples are npm test commands that support the stated testing purpose.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections are ordinary words inside descriptions, test names, or example method names. No hashing, encryption, cryptographic library, or security algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain prose and testing examples, not cryptographic operations. No evidence found for MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Reconnaissance Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections are testing prose about passing tests, reading an anti-pattern reference, and mocking behavior. No network scanning, host discovery, or system enumeration behavior appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is software testing guidance. No evidence found for network requests, port scans, filesystem enumeration, or environment probing.

content.value_statement: Teams often implement code before proving the expected behavior with tests. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through strict red-green-refactor cycles.
actual_capabilities: Defines when to use TDD for features, bug fixes, refactoring, and behavior changes.
Guides the red-green-refactor cycle with verification steps after each phase.
Provides examples of focused tests, minimal implementation, and over-engineering pitfalls.
Includes a checklist for confirming tests failed first and pass after implementation.
Provides anti-pattern guidance for mocks, test-only methods, and incomplete mock data.
limitations: Does not provide a runnable test framework or automation script.
Requires an existing project test command and test environment.
Uses strict TDD guidance that may not fit prototypes or generated code.
Does not replace human review for unclear product requirements.

use_cases.title: Feature Development
use_cases.description: Write the expected behavior as a failing test before implementing a new feature.

use_cases.title: Regression Fixes
use_cases.description: Reproduce a bug with a failing test, then implement the smallest fix that passes.

use_cases.title: Safer Refactoring
use_cases.description: Keep behavior stable while simplifying code under a passing test suite.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple TDD Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a small behavior change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-driven-development skill. Help me add one small behavior by writing the failing test first, verifying it fails, then implementing the minimum change.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Bug with a Regression Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-driven-development skill. Reproduce this bug with a failing test, confirm the failure reason, then guide the smallest fix and refactor step.

prompt_templates.title: Review Existing Tests for TDD Quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for test quality review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-driven-development and testing anti-pattern guidance. Review these tests for mock testing, test-only production methods, and missing red-phase proof.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multi-Step Refactor Safely
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for risky code changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-driven-development skill. Break this refactor into small red-green-refactor cycles, identify existing coverage gaps, and keep behavior unchanged.

output_examples.input: Add retry behavior to an operation that fails twice before succeeding.
output_examples.output: A focused RED test plan, expected failure check, minimal GREEN implementation guidance, and a final refactor checklist.

output_examples.input: Fix a form bug where empty email values are accepted.
output_examples.output: A regression test that proves the bug, a failure verification step, and a minimal validation change plan.

output_examples.input: Review tests that assert mock elements exist on the page.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis that the tests verify mocks instead of behavior, with guidance to test real user-visible behavior.

best_practices: Write one failing test for one behavior before changing production code.
Verify the test fails for the expected reason before implementing the fix.
Refactor only after all relevant tests pass with clean output.
anti_patterns: Writing implementation code first and adding tests after it works.
Testing mocks, stubs, or test-only helpers instead of real behavior.
Expanding scope during GREEN instead of implementing the smallest passing change.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It tells the assistant to use project test commands, but it does not include executable automation.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this only for unit tests?
faq.answer: No. The guidance can apply to unit, integration, and regression tests when the project supports them.

faq.question: Can this help with bug fixes?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends writing a failing regression test before changing the bugged behavior.

faq.question: When should teams skip this skill?
faq.answer: It may be unnecessary for throwaway prototypes, generated code, or simple configuration changes.

faq.question: Does it include security-sensitive behavior?
faq.answer: No evidence found. Manual review found documentation-only testing guidance and benign test command examples.
